subject: Sociology
title: How Sociology Began
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: How Sociology Began.

hook.text: Factories replaced farms. Cities swelled. Old social bonds shattered. Someone had to make sense of the chaos.

buildup.text: Sociology emerged in the 1800s as industrialization upended European life. The old order was gone.

discovery.text: Auguste Comte coined the term sociology in 1838, calling it the scientific study of society.

twist.text: Comte wanted sociology to be as rigorous as physics. Society, he believed, followed discoverable laws.

climax.text: The discipline was born from crisis — the need to understand a world that had changed beyond recognition.

punchline.text: Sociology was invented because the old world fell apart.

quiz.question: What historical event drove the creation of sociology?
options: The invention of the printing press
